tutorials.title: Section 1: Start Here
tutorials.description: Start Here plus the GitHub and WMS background pages that explain scope, navigation, and orientation before authors start the hands-on workflow.

tutorials.title: Need more information on GitHub?
tutorials.description: Explains the Oracle LiveLabs GitHub model, including repositories, forks, clones, and the safe authoring flow.

tutorials.title: Need to understand the status workflow in WMS?
tutorials.description: Summarizes the WMS lifecycle, the roles involved at each stage, and which detailed lab to use next.

tutorials.title: Section 2: Core Workshop Flow
tutorials.description: The required author workflow from workshop request through GitHub setup, repository sync, and Markdown content development.

tutorials.title: Lab 1: Submit new workshop in WMS
tutorials.description: Shows authors how to create a strong WMS request, understand the lifecycle, and keep workshop metadata accurate through development.

tutorials.title: Lab 2: Set up GitHub and install tools
tutorials.description: Covers the minimum GitHub, GitHub Desktop, VS Code, and preview setup needed for LiveLabs authoring.

tutorials.title: Lab 3: Stay in sync with GitHub environment
tutorials.description: Covers repository selection, fork and clone workflow, upstream sync, workshop structure, and GitHub Pages preview.

tutorials.title: Lab 5a: Develop Markdown and content
tutorials.description: Focuses on the core Markdown, manifest, security, validation, and reuse standards needed for production-ready workshop content.

tutorials.title: Section 3: Validation, QA, and Publish
tutorials.description: The production-readiness path for review, automated checks, self-QA, quarterly QA, and publishing.

tutorials.title: Lab 6a: QA checks and steps
tutorials.description: Explains how to share a reviewable preview, complete Self QA, and handle Quarterly QA after publish.

tutorials.title: Lab 6b: Pull Request Automated Checks
tutorials.description: This lab explains the automated checks that can block a pull request and how to interpret them.

tutorials.title: Lab 7: Publish your workshop
tutorials.description: Covers the final pull request and WMS publishing actions that move a completed workshop into production.

tutorials.title: Section 4: Workshop Components and Reuse
tutorials.description: Optional workshop building blocks and reusable components that extend the core author workflow.

tutorials.title: Lab 5b: Embedd SQL Developer using freesql.com
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to embed a FreeSQL editor directly into a workshop task.

tutorials.title: Lab 5c: Add LiveLabs Quizzes to your workshops
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to add interactive quizzes to a workshop.

tutorials.title: Section 5: Tools
tutorials.description: Support tools for screenshot creation, image cleanup, and related workshop-authoring tasks.

tutorials.title: Lab 4a: Capture effective screens - Best practices
tutorials.description: This lab describes the best practices for capturing workshop screenshots.

tutorials.title: Lab 4b: How to use OptiShot to optimize screenshots
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to use OptiShot to optimize workshop screenshots.

tutorials.title: Lab 5d: Fixomat 2000
tutorials.description: This lab explains how to use Fixomat for Markdown cleanup and image optimization before QA or publish.

tutorials.title: Section 6: Specialized Workflows and Reference
tutorials.description: Advanced, niche, and environment-specific workflows that are not part of the standard workshop-author path.

tutorials.title: Lab 9: Develop LiveLabs Sprints
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to create a Sprint for LiveLabs.

tutorials.title: Lab 10a: Setup Graphical Remote Desktop
tutorials.description: This lab describes how to create and use a graphical remote desktop for your workshop.

tutorials.title: Lab 10b: Create Custom Image for Marketplace
tutorials.description: This lab describes how to create and use a Marketplace image for your workshop.

tutorials.title: Lab 10c: Publish Your Image to Oracle Marketplace
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to publish your image to Oracle Marketplace.

tutorials.title: Lab 10d: Update the Image on Your Sandbox Environment
tutorials.description: This lab shows you how to get your new Marketplace image added to your LiveLab.

tutorials.title: Lab 11a: Secure Desktop Environments in LiveLabs
tutorials.description: This lab explains when OCI Secure Desktops make sense for LiveLabs workshops.

tutorials.title: Lab 11b: How to Request and Access Secure Desktop Environments
tutorials.description: This lab explains how to request and access OCI Secure Desktop environments.

tutorials.title: Section 7: Help, FAQ, and Support
tutorials.description: Support references, response expectations, and help-oriented documentation for LiveLabs workshop authors.

tutorials.title: Need Help?
tutorials.description: This lab explains where workshop authors should go for support, office hours, email help, and Slack assistance.

tutorials.title: LiveLabs FAQ
tutorials.description: This lab collects common questions about LiveLabs production, development, workshops, and event workflows.

tutorials.title: LiveLabs SLAs
tutorials.description: This document outlines response times for pull requests, approvals, publishing, and support.
